description:
You are a Site Reliability Engineer who just received a raw probe file that records
5-minute availability checks for several hosts.  
Your goal is to transform this raw data into two **strictly-formatted** report files
using nothing but standard shell utilities, in particular **awk** and **sed**
(plus mkdir / cp / mv if you really need them).

Initial data:
• Path: /home/user/data/uptime_checks.log  
• Encoding: UTF-8, Unix line-feeds (LF)  
• Format: one whitespace-separated record per line  
  1. ISO-8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g. 2024-05-20T00:00:00Z)  
  2. hostname (no spaces)  
  3. probe result, literally the string UP or DOWN  

Required outputs (the automated tests will read these exact files; paths,
filenames, line order, separators and numeric precision must match **exactly**).

1. Summary table  
   • Path: /home/user/reports/daily_uptime_summary_20240520.tsv  
   • Must start with a single header line, followed by one data line per host.  
   • Use the **tab character** (ASCII 0x09) as the field separator.
   • Columns, in this order:
     HOSTNAME, TOTAL_CHECKS, UP_COUNT, DOWN_COUNT, UPTIME_PERCENT  
       – TOTAL_CHECKS  = UP_COUNT + DOWN_COUNT  
       – UPTIME_PERCENT = (UP_COUNT / TOTAL_CHECKS) * 100  
         rounded to exactly **two** decimal places and suffixed with a percent sign.  
   • Sort the data lines alphabetically by HOSTNAME (ascending, case-sensitive).  
   • Example of the exact textual layout expected (→ represents a real tab, do not
     type the arrows or spaces):
     HOSTNAME→TOTAL_CHECKS→UP_COUNT→DOWN_COUNT→UPTIME_PERCENT  
     db-1→3→3→0→100.00%  
     web-1→3→2→1→66.67%  
     web-2→3→2→1→66.67%

2. Downtime intervals list  
   • Path: /home/user/reports/downtime_20240520.txt  
   • A plain space-delimited file (one space between each field, no tabs).  
   • Each line captures one *contiguous* period during which a host was DOWN.  
     Fields: HOSTNAME FIRST_DOWN_TIMESTAMP LAST_DOWN_TIMESTAMP  
     where LAST_DOWN_TIMESTAMP is the last timestamp **before** the next UP for
     the same host (or identical to FIRST_DOWN_TIMESTAMP when the outage lasts
     one single probe interval).  
   • The file should contain only the downtime lines, no header.  
   • Lines must appear in the chronological order of FIRST_DOWN_TIMESTAMP.  
   • Example of the exact textual layout expected:
     web-1 2024-05-20T00:05:00Z 2024-05-20T00:05:00Z  
     web-2 2024-05-20T00:25:00Z 2024-05-20T00:25:00Z

Operational constraints:
• Create the directory /home/user/reports if it does not already exist
  (you have no sudo; regular user permissions only).  
• Use awk and/or sed for all parsing and numeric work; avoid heavier tools
  (Python, Perl, jq, etc.).
• Do not alter the original /home/user/data/uptime_checks.log file.
• The command session should finish with both report files present and their
  contents exactly matching the formats described above.

Tip: it is often easier to compute per-host aggregates with awk’s associative
arrays, then post-process with sed to fine-tune layout.
Feel free to print intermediate diagnostics to the console, but the two report
files must remain clean and exactly formatted as specified.
truth:
Pre-existing input file (already on disk before the agent starts):

File: /home/user/data/uptime_checks.log
Contents (10 lines, each ending with LF):
2024-05-20T00:00:00Z web-1 UP
2024-05-20T00:05:00Z web-1 DOWN
2024-05-20T00:10:00Z web-1 UP
2024-05-20T00:15:00Z web-2 UP
2024-05-20T00:20:00Z web-2 UP
2024-05-20T00:25:00Z web-2 DOWN
2024-05-20T00:30:00Z db-1 UP
2024-05-20T00:35:00Z db-1 UP
2024-05-20T00:40:00Z db-1 UP

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/reports/daily_uptime_summary_20240520.tsv  
Exact byte-for-byte content (tabs shown here as literal \t for clarity,
real file contains ASCII 0x09 characters):

HOSTNAME\tTOTAL_CHECKS\tUP_COUNT\tDOWN_COUNT\tUPTIME_PERCENT\n
db-1\t3\t3\t0\t100.00%\n
web-1\t3\t2\t1\t66.67%\n
web-2\t3\t2\t1\t66.67%\n

2. /home/user/reports/downtime_20240520.txt  
Exact content (single spaces):

web-1 2024-05-20T00:05:00Z 2024-05-20T00:05:00Z\n
web-2 2024-05-20T00:25:00Z 2024-05-20T00:25:00Z\n
name: task_000000_56b13a16
